goal: Eval validation phase: 8/8 catalog complete + first-baseline results doc with two real findings driving Context Reliability v2

summary.title: 8/8 eval catalog + first-baseline results doc with two Context Reliability v2 findings
summary.what: Populate the three remaining stub fixtures (risk-aware-tests / docs-only-intent / refactor-cross-file) so the eval catalog is complete. Run the precondition scorer (mainline eval run): 6/8 pass, 2 honest failures. Ship docs_for_ai/eval-results.md capturing both findings as Context Reliability v2 inputs.
summary.why: Per the user directive (stop building capabilities, start running experiments): the eval has been the substrate for testing the product thesis since PR #69 / #81. With 5/8 fixtures it could not produce a first-baseline result; with 8/8 it can. The two failures are exactly the signal the eval is meant to produce: concrete retrieval gaps that drive v2.

decisions.chose: leave F1 + F2 as visible failures; document for v2
decisions.rationale: shipping retrieval fixes here would suppress the signal the eval exists to produce; the user explicitly asked to validate, not to keep adding capabilities

decisions.chose: fixtures express what an agent realistically needs; fail when retrieval cannot deliver
decisions.rationale: a fixture engineered to pass on todays scorer is a tautology; honest fixtures expose where retrieval still falls short

decisions.chose: first-baseline doc with per-fixture verdict + per-failure root cause + v2 candidate fix
decisions.rationale: gives the next-session author a self-contained brief; no need to re-derive findings from PR descriptions

risks: F1 and F2 are blockers for the LLM-runner round on those two fixtures specifically (the constraint cannot be acted on if it cant be retrieved). Mitigation: 6/8 fixtures still produce meaningful code-first vs intent-first comparisons; F1 + F2 unblock when v2 lands.
The honest 6/8 result reads as failure to readers who do not see the doc. Mitigation: results doc opens with what the eval is and is not, framing failures as inputs to v2.

anti_patterns.what: Engineering fixtures so they pass on todays scorer
anti_patterns.why: the fixture catalog must be aspirational-honest; engineering them to pass turns the eval into a tautology

anti_patterns.what: Shipping Context Reliability v2 fixes inline with the catalog completion
anti_patterns.why: mixes two design exercises and suppresses the signal the eval is built to produce; the user explicitly asked for validation before more building

anti_patterns.what: Skipping the eval-results.md doc and just merging the fixtures
anti_patterns.why: without the doc the failures look like authoring bugs; the doc is what makes the failures legible as v2 inputs

followups: Context Reliability v2 — F2: add anti_patterns to keyword search (SQLite intent_anti_patterns join + in-memory scorer)
Context Reliability v2 — F1: bypass relevance threshold for intents linked into the result via supersedes pinning
Re-run mainline eval run after v2; expect 8/8
Wire an LLM runner wrapper (anthropic / openai / local) and run code-first vs intent-first; update eval-results.md with the run table
If the LLM round shows intent-first matches code-first on most fixtures, design v2 of the eval (richer scoring, more fixture variants per scenario)
